creator: oyvindmo@initio.no
text:
New port: audio/ripit.  It's a perl-script aiding in encoding CDs to
MP3.  It does CDDB lookups, and supports encoding while reading from
CD.

Had to change it from using cdparanoia to using tosha, since
cdparanoia is Linux-only.  Is tosha the best CD-ripper to use as default?

I set /dev/rcd0c as default CD-ROM device.  I couldn't use /dev/cdrom,
because cda(1), which ripit uses, doesn't seem to handle symlinked
devices properly.
